description: Phase 0 complex-task baseline fixtures. Multi-obligation problems that exercise the conditions under which structured mode is meant to add value (Section 1.2): repeated same-goal stalls, missing intermediate lemmas, capability gaps, mutually-dependent definitions. The minimal core is expected to stall here, producing observable stagnation rather than silence. These fixtures are NOT meant to be fully solved; they anchor the structured-vs-minimal ablation.

tasks.proof_source:
theorem complex_two_obligations (n : Nat) (h : n ≥ 2) : 2 * n ≥ n + 1 ∧ n * n ≥ n := by
  {{proof}}
tasks.why_complex: Two conjuncts: a single proposal must discharge both obligations or the loop will repeatedly stall on the second goal.

tasks.proof_source:
theorem complex_missing_intermediate (x : ℝ) (h : x > 1) : Real.log x > 0 := by
  {{proof}}
tasks.why_complex: Requires a specific Mathlib lemma (Real.pos_log / Real.log_pos) whose exact name and signature are not obvious; capability-audit territory.
